questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A small pond of depth 0.5 m deep is exposed to a cold winter with outside temperature of 263 K . Thermal conductivity of ice is $K=2.2 \mathrm{Wm}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$, latent heat $L=3.4 \times 10^5 \mathrm{Jkg}^{-1}$ and density $\rho=0.9 \times 10^3 \mathrm{kgm}^{-3}$. Take the temperature of the pond to be 273 K . The time taken for the whole pond to freeze is about

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two identical rods made of two different metals A and B with thermal conductivities $K_{\mathrm{A}}$ and $K_{\mathrm{B}}$ respectively are joined end to end. The free end of A is kept at a temperature $T_1$ while the free end of B is kept at a temperature $T_2\left( < \mathrm{T}_1\right)$. Therefore, in the steady state
options: the temperature of the junction will be determined only by $K_{\mathrm{A}}$ and $K_{\mathrm{B}}$.
if the lengths of the rods are doubled the rate of heat flow will be halved.
if the temperatures at the two free ends are interchanged the junction temperature will change.
the composite rod has an equivalent thermal conductivity of $\frac{2 K_A K_B}{K_A+K_B}$.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A steel cooking pan has copper coating at its bottom. The thickness of copper coating is half the thickness of steel bottom. The conductivity of copper is three times that of steel. If the temperature of blue flame is $119^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and that of the interior of the cooking pan is $91^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, then the temperature at the interface between the steel bottom and the copper coating in the steady state is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Two identical rooms in a house are connected by an open doorway. The temperatures in the two rooms are maintained at two different values. Therefore,
options: The room with higher temperature contains more amount of air.
The room with lower temperature contains more amount of air.
Both the rooms contain the same amount of air.
The room with higher pressure contains more amount of air

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Three containers $A, B$ and $C$ are filled with water at different temperature. When 1 litre of water from A is mixed with 2 litre of water from B , the resulting temperature of mixture is $52^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. When 1 litre of water from B is mixed with 2 litre of water from C , the resulting temperature of mixture is $40^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Similarly when 1 litre of water from C is mixed with 2 litre of water from A , the resulting temperature of mixture is $34^0 \mathrm{C}$. Temperature of mixture when one litre of water from each container is mixed (neglect the water equivalent of container) is

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: Solar constant for Earth is 2.0 cal per $\mathrm{cm}^2$ per minute. $[1 \mathrm{cal}=4.2 \mathrm{~J}]$. Angular diameter of the Sun (as seen from the Earth) is $\frac{1^{\circ}}{2}$ (= half a degree). Treating Sun as a black body, its surface temperature is estimated to be nearly

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: A certain rod of uniform area of cross section $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{A}=1.0 \mathrm{~cm}^2\right)$ with its length $=2 \mathrm{~m}$ is thermally insulated on its lateral surface. The thermal conductivity $(K)$ of the material of the rod varies with temperature $T$ as $K=\frac{\alpha}{T}$ where $\alpha$ is a constant. The two ends of the rod are maintained at temperature of $T_1=90^{\circ}$. and $T_2=10^{\circ} \ldots$ The correct option(s) is /are
options: The temperature at 50 cm from the colder end is $17.32^{\circ}$
The temperature at 50 cm from the hotter end is $51.96^{\circ}$
The rate of heat flow per unit area of cross section of the rod is $1.1 \alpha$ in SI units.
The temperature gradient is numerically higher near the hot end compare to that near the cold end.

questions.subject: NSEP
questions.q: End A of a uniform thin rod of length 2 L is in boiling water $\left(100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right)$ and end B is in melting ice $\left(0^{\circ} \mathrm{C}\right) . \mathrm{P}$ and Q are two points at distance $\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}$ from A and B respectively. A similar bent rod of length $\frac{3 \mathrm{~L}}{2}$ of same material and equal cross section is joined to $\operatorname{rod} \mathrm{AB}$ between points $P$ and $Q$ as shown in figure. Then
options: Temperature at P will increase and that at Q will decrease
Rate of flow of heat will increase by $25 \%$
Rate of flow of heat will decrease by $20 \%$
Rate of heat flow will increase by $37.5 \%$
